title: Remotes, Tracking, Fetch, Pull, and Push · Repository Repair Bay
summary: Synchronize with a shared repository while separating network transfer, local integration, authentication, and authorization decisions. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.
Fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.
Explain pull as fetch plus configured integration and select merge, rebase, or fast-forward-only deliberately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Local main is 3 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.

Inspect the Remote Reference Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish remote names, urls, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.

content.text: For Remote Reference Model, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Remote Reference Model precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Local main is 3 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: origin is a special central repository that Git discovers automatically.

lines.label: assumption

Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: origin is a special central repository that Git discovers automatically.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations. Artifact under review: a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Remote Reference Model, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Remote Reference Model record says a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Remote Reference Model without its stated preconditions, omit the a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Remote Reference Model for a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook.

Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Preview source and destination refs, upstream setup, force protections, tags, and deletion semantics before push.

Which observation would disprove this suspected cause: “git push always publishes the currently checked-out branch to a branch with the same name.”?

steps.title: Credential Transport · fault isolation
steps.instruction:
debug case — Local main is 3 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.

Which observation would disprove this suspected cause: “A successful SSH connection proves permission to push every branch.”?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate https or ssh transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-credential-transport-h4heo5" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Credential Transport into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-credential-transport-h4heo5
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Credential Transport structural check is only one gate. a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record

Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push. Competency probe: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.
steps.why: Retrieving Credential Transport through the concrete a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.
